steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The question asks for the best description of the text's overall structure. Map the passage in order: (1) a study is introduced with survey data from two cities, (2) the results are stated (53.5% vs. 44.7%), (3) a possible explanation is raised (nonpark amenities), and (4) that explanation is rejected.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — An unexpected finding is described, and then that finding is attributed to the method used to collect survey data in Panama City and Caracas.: The passage never questions or credits the data-collection method. ✗
  • Choice B — An unresolved question about park planning is described, and then examples from a study of Panama City and Caracas are used to answer that question.: The passage opens with a study description, not an unresolved question. ✗
  • Choice C — A study involving surveys in Panama City and Caracas is introduced, and then a possible explanation for some of the results is considered and rejected.: Matches the passage exactly — study introduced, results given, nonpark-amenity explanation raised and then refuted. ✓
  • Choice D — A study of park use in Panama City is described, and then a similar study of park use in Caracas is analyzed to corroborate the findings of the Panama City study.: Both cities are part of one single study; no second study corroborates the first. ✗
That confirms C is correct.
